title: A note for yourself six months from now
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the page instead of leaving a note in it. Bring the code back.
hint2.empty: Restore the page from the last lesson and add the comment line.
why.not-closed: Some tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order.
hint2.not-closed: Check the comment too — it also has a beginning and an end.
why.no-comment: There is no comment. The markers are odd and hard to remember: `<!--` in front, `-->` behind.
hint2.no-comment: Put it somewhere in `<body>` — above the image, for instance.
hint3.no-comment: The whole line is `<!-- some text -->`. Look at the example.
why.not-closed-comment: You opened the comment but never closed it. `-->` is missing. An unclosed comment eats everything after it — the whole page disappears.
hint2.not-closed-comment: Look at where your comment ends.
hint3.not-closed-comment: Without the closing `-->` the browser assumes the comment runs to the end of the file.
why.lost-content: Something disappeared from the page. You are adding a comment, not deleting. And watch out: you may have commented it out — and commented-out things do not exist.
hint2.lost-content: Check whether something ended up INSIDE the comment. Between `<!--` and `-->` nothing exists.
why.lost-img: The image is gone. You may have wrapped it in the comment. Commented-out code does not exist — as far as the browser is concerned it is not there.
hint2.lost-img: Look at where your comment ends. If `-->` is below the image line, the image is inside.
blocks.1.text: Your code is getting long. A dozen lines doing different things. Today it is all clear to you.
blocks.2.text: Six months from now you will open it and think: "what is this, who wrote it, why is it like this". You wrote it. You just forgot.
blocks.5.text: The markers are odd and hard to remember. `<!--` in front, `-->` behind. Notice the exclamation mark is only in the opening one — the closing one does not have it.
blocks.6.text: Put a comment in your code, anywhere. Run it. Nothing will appear on the page.
blocks.7.text: It is not a hiding place
blocks.8.text: A comment is not displayed, but it travels to the visitor along with the rest of the page. Right-click, View Page Source — and there it is, word for word. Anyone can read it.
blocks.9.text: This is not a minor detail. OWASP — the organisation that writes the security handbooks — has a dedicated procedure for reviewing comments for leaked information. The reason: people put debugging notes in them and then forget to remove them.
blocks.10.items.0: No passwords or keys. It sounds obvious and it is a classic.
blocks.10.items.1: No internal addresses or server names.
blocks.10.items.2: No notes about unfinished things that have not been announced.
blocks.12.text: The second use is the more common one: you disable something temporarily without deleting it.
blocks.14.text: The image disappears from the page. It is not deleted — as far as the browser is concerned it is not there. Remove the markers and it comes back. You will do this constantly while hunting for where a bug comes from.
blocks.15.text: But do not fool yourself that you removed it. The file still contains that line and it still travels across the network. Hidden does not mean gone.
blocks.18.text: Comments do not nest. Here the whole comment ends at the first `-->`, and the remainder — `still outer -->` — shows up on the page as ordinary text. It looks like a bug out of nowhere.
blocks.19.text: The second: avoid two hyphens side by side inside a comment. `--` can terminate it early and you get the same result. If you need a dash, use one.
blocks.21.text: Add one comment to your page. Anything at all — but make it something that would actually help you half a year from now.
blocks.22.text: "This is the heading" does not help. "The picture is temporary until I take my own" does.
blocks.24.items.0: `<!--` opens, `-->` closes. The exclamation mark is only in front.
blocks.24.items.1: It does not show on the page, but it is visible in View Source.
blocks.24.items.2: No passwords or internal notes inside.
blocks.24.items.3: Commented-out code does not run, but it still travels.
blocks.24.items.4: Comments inside comments do not work.
walkthrough.0.text: One line above the image. Nothing else is touched.
walkthrough.1.text: The note says why the image is what it is, not what it is. The second is visible from the code itself; the first is not.
walkthrough.2.text: If your page suddenly goes blank, check the comments first. A missing `-->` turns the whole rest of the file into a note.
